rationale: The citing paper does more than list Ding (2007) as background; it uses Ding's ICBC mechanism as a core method in its experimental design. The authors explicitly state they focus on Ding's approach because it can be implemented with one real product, and they report that their ICBC treatment uses the same incentive-alignment mechanism Ding proposes. They also interpret key empirical patterns, such as higher price sensitivity under incentive alignment, in light of Ding's prior findings. This indicates meaningful methodological dependence rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies Ding (2007)'s incentive-aligned conjoint mechanism to construct its ICBC condition and evaluate willingness-to-pay measurement in a new empirical setting. It uses Ding's procedure as the operative method and compares its performance against OE, CBC, BDM, and real purchase benchmark data.
